posts.title: Credential Lifecycle 101: From Issuance to Expiry, and Who Owns Each Stage
posts.description: From identity proofing to renewal, the long-term success of a verifiable digital credential program depends on clear ownership at every step.

posts.title: What Is a Credential Format, and Why Does It Matter Which One You Choose?
posts.description: The format of a digital credential may seem like a technical detail, but it can shape interoperability, privacy, and flexibility for years to come.

posts.title: What Is a Presentation Request, and Who Controls What Gets Asked For?
posts.description: Explores how presentation request rules determine what information can be requested and resident control mechanisms.

posts.title: What Does 'Resident-Centric' Actually Mean in a Digital Identity System?
posts.description: Resident-centric digital identity is not a marketing claim, it is a set of design decisions that determine who controls data, privacy, and participation.

posts.title: What Is the Difference Between Authentication and Identity Proofing?
posts.description: Explains how systems must verify both identity and credential ownership before establishing trust.

posts.title: SpruceID at the 2026 Utah Data Governance Summit
posts.description: SpruceID demonstrated how marriage licensing can serve as a scalable digital trust model.

posts.title: What Is a Digital Signature, and Why Does It Matter for Government Credentials?
posts.description: Explores how cryptographic signatures function similarly to physical security features in digital credentials.

posts.title: What Does 'Open Source' Mean for a Government Digital Identity System?
posts.description: Open source increases transparency when paired with governance and long-term support structures.

posts.title: What Is the Difference Between Remote and In-Person Identity Proofing?
posts.description: Examines how agencies decide residents will verify their identity before accessing digital services.

posts.title: What Happens to Your Digital ID If You Lose Your Phone?
posts.description: Well-designed credentials ensure phone loss doesn't result in loss of identity control.

posts.title: How Do Verifiable Digital Credentials Support Audit and Compliance Requirements?
posts.description: Credentials enable agencies to embed audit readiness into service delivery processes.

posts.title: Portable Credentials: What the Term Means in Practice
posts.description: Digital identity should work across jurisdictions and boundaries to achieve practical portability.

posts.title: SpruceID at the SEC Roundtable: Advancing Privacy-First Digital Identity
posts.description: SpruceID's participation in an SEC roundtable focused on privacy-first identity solutions.

posts.title: Digital Identity In America: Series Overview
posts.description: Overview of a series exploring digital identity topics in America.
